steps.title: Fastest path: use the triangle relationships first
steps.content: Label the equal legs as $a$ and use the special right-triangle ratio to connect the legs to the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Isosceles right triangle relationships
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle with equal legs of length $a$, the hypotenuse is $a\sqrt{2}$ by the Pythagorean theorem. The perimeter is:
$$a + a + a\sqrt{2} = a(2 + \sqrt{2})$$

steps.title: Backsolving with choice C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (20): If the hypotenuse is 20, then since the hypotenuse equals $a\sqrt{2}$, we have $a = \frac{20}{\sqrt{2}} = 10\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is:
$$2(10\sqrt{2}) + 20 = 20\sqrt{2} + 20 = 20 + 20\sqrt{2}$$
This matches the given perimeter exactly. That confirms C is correct.
